description:
You are working as an artifact–curation engineer who is preparing a small
    binary repository for three open-source optimisation solvers.  
    A staging area that already contains the three archives has been placed
    at the absolute path  
      /home/user/curation/binaries  

    The archives are (exact, case-sensitive names):

        cbc-2.10.5-linux-x86_64.tar.gz
        glpk-5.0-linux-x86_64.tar.gz
        scip-8.0.3-linux-x86_64.tar.gz

    Your manager wants an **inventory log** that captures the name of each
    archive together with its byte-size so the files can later be uploaded to
    an external artefact store.

    Expected steps (3–4 terminal commands total):

      1. Make sure the directory /home/user/curation/logs exists.
      2. Determine the exact size (in bytes) of every “*.tar.gz” file that is
         currently present in /home/user/curation/binaries.
      3. Create a new file called
            /home/user/curation/logs/solver_inventory.csv
         and write the inventory information to it using the following
         **strict CSV format**:

             • The very first line MUST be the header exactly:
                   solver_id,archive,size_bytes
             • Each subsequent line MUST follow the pattern:
                   <solver_id>,<archive_name>,<size_in_bytes>
             • <solver_id> is the lowercase solver name (cbc, glpk, scip).
             • The file MUST contain exactly one line per archive and MUST be
               sorted in **alphabetical order by <solver_id>**.
             • Do NOT include any directory path in <archive_name>.
             • There must be a single trailing newline at the end of the file.

    Example of the required final appearance (sizes are only examples; the
    test-suite will verify the real values):

        solver_id,archive,size_bytes
        cbc,cbc-2.10.5-linux-x86_64.tar.gz,31
        glpk,glpk-5.0-linux-x86_64.tar.gz,29
        scip,scip-8.0.3-linux-x86_64.tar.gz,31

    The automated grader will:

      • Check that /home/user/curation/logs/solver_inventory.csv exists.
      • Confirm the header line is correct.
      • Validate that the three data lines are present, alphabetically sorted,
        and that each reported “size_bytes” value exactly matches the actual
        byte-length of the corresponding archive in
        /home/user/curation/binaries.
      • Ensure there are no extra or missing lines.

    You do not need to extract or analyse the archives themselves; simply
    produce the correctly formatted inventory file.
truth:
The repository already contains the three archives with the following
    precise contents (each ends with a single ASCII newline “\n”):

      1. /home/user/curation/binaries/cbc-2.10.5-linux-x86_64.tar.gz  
         Content string: "CBC binary placeholder v2.10.5\n"  
         Byte size: 31

      2. /home/user/curation/binaries/glpk-5.0-linux-x86_64.tar.gz  
         Content string: "GLPK binary placeholder v5.0\n"  
         Byte size: 29

      3. /home/user/curation/binaries/scip-8.0.3-linux-x86_64.tar.gz  
         Content string: "SCIP binary placeholder v8.0.3\n"  
         Byte size: 31

    The expected, fully correct CSV file that the agent must produce is:

        solver_id,archive,size_bytes
        cbc,cbc-2.10.5-linux-x86_64.tar.gz,31
        glpk,glpk-5.0-linux-x86_64.tar.gz,29
        scip,scip-8.0.3-linux-x86_64.tar.gz,31\n

    (There is exactly one terminal newline character after the final “31”.)
name: task_000000_1102c710
